questions.question: Explain the key differences between data parallelism and task parallelism with examples.
keyPoints: Data parallelism applies same operation to multiple data elements (e.g., image processing)
Task parallelism executes different operations concurrently (e.g., web server handling multiple requests)
Data parallelism requires decomposable data, task parallelism requires independent tasks
Different implementation approaches and optimization strategies

questions.question: Compare and contrast the MapReduce and Actor model paradigms, discussing their strengths, weaknesses, and ideal use cases.
keyPoints: MapReduce: batch processing, data-intensive applications, simple programming model
Actor Model: concurrent message-driven systems, stateful applications, complex coordination
MapReduce strengths: scalability, fault tolerance, simplicity
Actor Model strengths: responsiveness, encapsulation, fault isolation
MapReduce weaknesses: latency, iterative processing challenges
Actor Model weaknesses: complexity, message overhead
Use cases: MapReduce for analytics, Actor Model for reactive systems
